name: infinitode-py

metadata.name: infinitode.py
metadata.summary: A python wrapper for the Infinitode 2 API.

documentation:
# Infinitode.py

An asynchronous Python wrapper for the Infinitode-2 API using `async`-`await` syntax.

---

## Installation

Install the library via pip:

```bash
pip install infinitode.py
```

---

## Showcase

### Setting Up a Session

Create a session to communicate with Rainy's API. Use the `with` statement to ensure the session closes properly.
Alternatively you can can simply call `Session.close()` once you don't need it anymore.

```python
import asyncio
import infinitode

async def main():
    async with infinitode.Session() as API:
        # Your API calls go here
        pass

asyncio.run(main())
```

---

### Fetching Leaderboard Data

Parameters:
- `mapname`: The map identifier (e.g., "5.1" or "4.b1" or "zecred").
- `playerid` (sometimes optional): The player's unique identifier.
- `mode` (optional): One of `'score'`, `'waves'`. Defaults to `'score'`.
- `difficulty` (optional): One of `'EASY'`, `'NORMAL'`, `'ENDLESS_I'`. Defaults to `'NORMAL'`.

#### Player Score on a Specific Map

Retrieve the score of a specific player on a particular map.

```python
score_5_1 = await API.leaderboards_rank(
    mapname="5.1",
    playerid="U-E9BP-FSN9-H6ENMQ",
    mode="score",
    difficulty="NORMAL"
)
```

#### Top 200 Leaderboard on a Specific Map

Retrieve the leaderboard of the top 200 wave scores on 5.1.

```python
leaderboard_5_1 = await API.leaderboards(
    mapname=5.1,
    mode="waves"
)
```

#### Runtime Leaderboard

Fetch the leaderboard displayed during gameplay. This provides additional percentile information.

```python
runtime_5_1 = await API.runtime_leaderboards(
    mapname=5.1,
    playerid="U-E9BP-FSN9-H6ENMQ",
    difficulty="ENDLESS_I"
)
```

---

#### Skill Point Leaderboard

Get the top 3 skill point owners. Optionally specify a `playerid`.

```python
sp_leaderboard = await API.skill_point_leaderboard(
    playerid="U-E9BP-FSN9-H6ENMQ"
)
```


#### Daily Quest Leaderboard

Retrieve the top 200 players of today's daily quest or a specified date.

- `date`: `YYYY-MM-DD` (string) or `datetime.datetime`.

```python
dq_leaderboard = await API.daily_quest_leaderboards("2024-12-05")
```

---

### Working with Leaderboards

A leaderboard is a sequence of scores with additional utility functions.

#### Access Score and Leaderboard Attributes

Leaderboards always have these attributes:
- `mapname`, `mode`, `difficulty`, `total`

And optionally these:
- `date`, `season`, `player` (will return a score object)

Scores always have these attributes:
- `playerid`, `rank`, `score`, `mapname`, `mode`, `difficulty` 

And optionally these:
- `has_pfp`, `level`, `nickname`, `pinned_badge`, `position`, `top`, `total`, `player` (will return a Player object if fetched beforehand)

#### Example for printing attributes, scores, and leaderboards

```python
for score in leaderboard_5_1:
    print(score.nickname)
    score.print_score()  # Helper method

leaderboard_5_1.print_scores()
```

#### Slice a Leaderboard

Retrieve a subset of the leaderboard:

```python
top_1 = leaderboard_5_1[0]
top_10 = leaderboard_5_1[0:10]
print(len(top_10))
```

---

### Player Information

#### Fetch Player Data

Retrieve detailed player information.

```python
player = await API.player("U-E9BP-FSN9-H6ENMQ")
```

Attributes include:
- `playerid`, `nickname`, `level`, `xp`, `season_level`, `total_score`, and many more.

#### Get Player Scores

Use the `score` method to access specific map scores.

```python
player.score(5.1).print_score()
```

#### Fetch Daily Quest and Skill Point Scores

Use additional API calls to retrieve daily quest and skill point scores:

```python
await player.fetch_daily_quest(API)
await player.fetch_skill_point(API)

print(player.daily_quest.rank, player.skill_point.score)
```

---

### Beta Scores

Almost all API calls support an additional `beta` boolean parameter. This will make a request to the beta servers instead. No guarantees for it working.

```python
leaderboard_5_1 = await API.leaderboards(mapname=5.1, mode='waves', beta=True)
```


### Logging

Set up logging to monitor requests and responses. 

- `INFO`: Logs every request.
- `DEBUG`: Logs server responses.

```python
import infinitode
import asyncio
import logging

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)

async def main():
    async with infinitode.Session() as API:
        await API.leaderboards("6.3")

asyncio.run(main())
```

---

### Notes

- Some data (e.g., seasonal leaderboard, players) require additional parsing and will take longer to process.
- Do not abuse this API wrapper for any kind of malicious action.

classes.name: infinitode.errors.InfinitodeError
documentation: Base Infinitode Error.

classes.name: infinitode.errors.APIError
documentation: Error directly related to the communication with the API.

classes.name: infinitode.errors.BadArgument
documentation: Error raised when an invalid argument is passed.

modules.name: infinitode
documentation:
Infinitode API Wrapper
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

An asynchronous wrapper for the Infinitode API.

functions.name: infinitode.utils.async_expiring_cache
documentation:
Decorator to cache coroutines return value for the given amount of seconds.

It is not perfect because

methods.name: infinitode.utils._MissingSentinel.__eq__
documentation: taken from discord.py, see:
https://github.com/Rapptz/discord.py/blob/a14b43f2fda863ed6555374eb872bf014bdd1adf/discord/utils.py#L96

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.playerid
documentation: The player's playerid.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.nickname
documentation: The player's nickname.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.level
documentation: The player's XP level.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.xp
documentation: The player's XP in the current level.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.xp_max
documentation: The XP required for the player to level up.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.season_level
documentation: The player's season XP level.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.season_xp
documentation: The player's XP in the current season level.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.season_xp_max
documentation: The season XP required for the player to level up.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.badges
documentation:
The player's badges as a dict in the form:
    type: (rarity, colour)

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.total_score
documentation: The player's total (seasonal) score.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.total_rank
documentation: The player's total (seasonal) rank (placement in the leaderboard).

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.total_top
documentation: The player's total (seasonal) top % (as str).

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.replays
documentation: The player's amount of verified replays.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.issues
documentation: The player's amount of replays that failed verification.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.created_at
documentation: The player's creation date as a str in the format: '%Y-%m-%d'.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.avatar_link
documentation: The link to the player's avatar. Invalid URL if the user doesn't have a pfp.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.daily_quest
documentation: Returns the player's daily quest score, or raises InfinitodeError if it wasn't fetched yet.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.skill_point
documentation: Returns the player's daily quest score, or raises InfinitodeError if it wasn't fetched yet.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.score
documentation: Returns the player's score on the given map.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.fetch_daily_quest
documentation:
Fetches the player's Daily Quest score if it wasn't fetched already.
Returns None if the player is not ranked.

methods.name: infinitode.player.Player.fetch_skill_point
documentation:
Fetches the player's Skill Point score if it wasn't fetched already.
Returns None if the player is not ranked.

documentation: magic methods
Represents an in-game Player.

documentation: The name of the method responsible for creating this Score.

documentation: The name of the map this score is from.

documentation: The playerid of the player.

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.rank
documentation: The rank (position) of this score.

documentation: The score (points) of this score.

documentation: Whether the player has a pfp or not. Only sometimes available.

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.level
documentation: The XP level of the player. Only sometimes available.

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.nickname
documentation: The nickname of the player. Only sometimes available.

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.pinned_badge
documentation: The pinned badge of the player. Only sometimes available.

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.position
documentation: Similar to ~.rank, but coming from the server. Only sometimes available and unreliable beyond top 200.

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.top
documentation: The top % of this score (as str).

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.player
documentation: The player object of this score. This has to be fetched first using the ~.fetch_player coro.

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.fetch_player
documentation: Fetch the player using a given session (This is an API call).

documentation: Formats the score the way i use it in my Advinas bot.

methods.name: infinitode.score.Score.print_score
documentation: Prints out the result of format_score().

documentation: magic methods
Represents a single in-game Score.

documentation: The name of the map this leaderboard is from.

documentation: The mode of the leaderboard (score/waves).

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.difficulty
documentation: The difficulty of the leaderboard (EASY/NORMAL/ENDLESS_I)

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.total
documentation: The total amount of players on this map.

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.date
documentation: The date this leaderboard is from. Only exists when the method is daily_quest_leaderboards.

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.season
documentation: The current season which this leaderboard is from. Only exists when the method is seasonal_leaderboard.

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.player
documentation: The score of the player. Only exists when a valid playerid was specified.

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.is_empty
documentation: Whether there are no scores saved in this leaderboard or there are.

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.format_scores
documentation: Default format used in my Advinas Bot. To format the scores yourself, iterate over this object.

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.print_scores
documentation: Prints out the result of format_scores().

methods.name: infinitode.leaderboard.Leaderboard.get_score
documentation: Returns the score in the leaderboard where the given attribute equals the given value.

functions.name: infinitode.core._base_url
documentation: fmt: on

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.__aenter__
documentation: async enter and exit allow for the fancy "with" statements
useful so you don't have to close the session yourself

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.close
documentation: Closes the internal ClientSession.

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session._kwarg_check
documentation: Rough parameter checking, "*" makes params keyword only

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session._post
documentation: Internal post method to communicate with Rainy's API
not being more specific with the payload type
so the typechecker stops annoying me

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.leaderboards_rank
documentation:
Retrieves a Score of the given player.
A valid playerid needs to be specified.

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.leaderboards
documentation:
Retrieves a Leaderboard.
The leaderboard contains the top 200 scores of the specified map.

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.runtime_leaderboards
documentation:
Retrieves a Runtime Leaderboard (The one displayed top right in-game).
A valid playerid needs to be specified.
The leaderboard contains the top 200 scores and one Score for each top% of the specified map.

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.skill_point_leaderboard
documentation:
Retrieves the Skill Point Leaderboard.
The leaderboard contains the top 3 skill point owners (looking at you, Eupho!).

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.daily_quest_leaderboards
documentation:
Retrieves the Daily Quest Leaderboard for the given date.
If an invalid or no date is provided, the date will be set to the current date.
You may disable the invalid date warning by setting the warning param to False.
The leaderboard contains the top 200 DQ players of the given date.

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.seasonal_leaderboard
documentation:
Retrieves the season Leaderboard.
The leaderboard contains the top 100 scores in the season.
This coroutine never takes arguments.

methods.name: infinitode.core.Session.player
documentation:
Retrieves the Player.
A valid playerid needs to be specified.
